promise_text_en: To ensure the balanced distribution of administrative facilities, education, healthcare, employment, and industrial growth, the Naam Tamilar government will decentralize the state capital.
promise_text_ta: நிருவாக வசதிக்காக, கல்வி, மருத்துவம், வேலைவாய்ப்பு, தொழில் வளர்ச்சி உட்பட அனைத்தும் பரவலாகச் சென்றடைய, நாம் தமிழர் அரசு தலைநகரை பரவலாக்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 33
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to rebalance regional development and reduce over-concentration in Chennai by distributing state functions across multiple cities.

promise_components.component: Decentralize the state capital by distributing administrative, educational, healthcare, employment, and industrial functions.
promise_components.analysis: This is a high-level statement of intent for a massive, multi-year capital project. The cost would involve constructing new administrative complexes, relocating personnel, and developing new infrastructure. The reference data does not contain figures for capital expenditure on this scale.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The cost of land acquisition, construction of multiple government complexes, and relocation of thousands of employees would run into tens of thousands of crores, far exceeding the annual discretionary budget.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a fundamental restructuring of the state's governance model to address the structural problem of regional imbalance. If successful, it would permanently alter the state's economic and administrative geography by creating multiple growth poles.

promise_text_en: Tiruchirappalli (Trichy), the geographical center of Tamil Nadu, will be made the primary Administrative Capital, housing the Secretariat, Legislative Assembly, Revenue Department, and the State Planning Commission.
promise_text_ta: தமிழகத்தின் மையப்புள்ளியான திருச்சி, தலைமைச் செயலகம், சட்டமன்றம், வருவாய்த்துறை மற்றும் மாநிலத் திட்டக் குழுவுடன் முதன்மை நிர்வாகத் தலைநகரமாகச் செயல்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 34
impact_mechanism: This promise seeks to improve administrative accessibility for the whole state by relocating the core of government to a geographically central location.

promise_components.component: Designate Tiruchirappalli (Trichy) as the primary Administrative Capital.
promise_components.analysis: This involves moving the Secretariat, Legislative Assembly, Revenue Department, and State Planning Commission to Trichy. The cost includes constructing a new Secretariat complex, Assembly building, and departmental offices, plus personnel relocation. No data is available for these large-scale construction and relocation costs.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. This is a capital expenditure project, not a recurring annual cost. The cost would be extremely high, likely in the tens of thousands of crores.
sustainability_reasoning: By moving the seat of government, the promise aims to re-center political and administrative power away from the periphery. This is a permanent structural change intended to foster more balanced development across all regions of the state.

promise_text_en: Chennai will be designated as the Tech Capital, reducing its administrative load to become the hub for Information Technology, media, port and maritime commerce, and financial services.
promise_text_ta: சென்னை, தனது நிருவாகப் பளுவைக் குறைத்துக் கொண்டு, தகவல் தொழில்நுட்பம், திரைக்கலை, துறைமுகம் மற்றும் நிதிச் சேவைகளின் மையமாக, ஒரு தொழில்நுட்பத் தலைநகரமாக (Tech Capital) செயல்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 34
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to reinforce Chennai's economic strengths by formally branding it as the hub for technology and commerce.

promise_components.component: Designate Chennai as the Tech Capital, focusing on IT, media, port/maritime commerce, and financial services.
promise_components.analysis: This is a policy branding and focus exercise. Chennai is already a major hub for these sectors. The promise formalizes this status, and any cost would depend on unspecified new incentives or infrastructure projects.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. This is a policy declaration with no specific, quantifiable financial commitment mentioned.
sustainability_reasoning: Chennai is already the de facto capital for these industries. This promise formalizes a label and provides policy direction but does not create a new structural reality; it primarily rebrands the existing one.
coverage_gap_note: This focuses on specific modern industries, potentially leaving traditional sectors within the city unaddressed.

promise_text_en: Coimbatore will function as the Industrial & Business Capital, serving as the backbone of the state's manufacturing sector and encouraging entrepreneurship in the western region.
promise_text_ta: கோவை, மாநிலத்தின் உற்பத்தித் துறையின் முதுகெலும்பாக, தொழில் மற்றும் வணிகத் தலைநகரமாக (Industrial & Business Capital) செயல்படும். இது மேற்கு மண்டலத்தின் தொழில் முனைவோரை ஊக்குவிக்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 34
impact_mechanism: This promise seeks to boost manufacturing in the state's western region by formally recognizing Coimbatore as its industrial center.

promise_components.component: Designate Coimbatore as the Industrial & Business Capital, focusing on manufacturing and entrepreneurship.
promise_components.analysis: This formalizes Coimbatore's existing role as a major industrial and textile hub ('Manchester of South India'). The cost would depend on unspecified new policies or incentives to further boost these sectors.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. This is a policy declaration with no specific financial commitment mentioned.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a branding exercise that leverages Coimbatore's established industrial character. It signals a focus but doesn't propose a new structural intervention, instead formalizing an existing identity.
coverage_gap_note: The focus is on large-scale industry and business, potentially overlooking the needs of small and micro-enterprises in the region.

promise_text_en: Madurai will be designated as the Cultural & Heritage Capital, serving as the center for Tamil identity and justice. It will house the Tamil Development Department and focus on tourism, heritage, and the preservation of arts.
promise_text_ta: மதுரை, தமிழின் அடையாளம் மற்றும் நீதிக்கான மையமாக, மொழி, கலை மற்றும் பண்பாட்டுத் தலைநகரமாக (Cultural & Heritage Capital) திகழும். இது தமிழ் வளர்ச்சித் துறை, சுற்றுலா மற்றும் கலைகளின் பாதுகாப்பு மையமாக விளங்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 35
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to promote tourism and Tamil culture by centralizing heritage-related state functions in Madurai.

promise_components.component: Designate Madurai as the Cultural & Heritage Capital, housing the Tamil Development Department.
promise_components.analysis: Madurai is already a major cultural and religious center. Moving the Tamil Development Department is a concrete but relatively small-scale action. The cost involves relocating one department and any potential new funding for unspecified cultural initiatives.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The relocation cost for one department is minor, but any new tourism or heritage project costs are unknown.
sustainability_reasoning: This leverages Madurai's deep historical roots and is a sensible branding strategy. While moving a department is a tangible action, the overall promise is largely a formalization of Madurai's existing cultural significance rather than a structural change.
coverage_gap_note: Focuses on heritage and arts, which may not address the broader economic development needs of the southern districts.

promise_text_en: Kanyakumari will be established as the Epistemological Capital, serving as the center for the state's long-term vision, natural resource protection, environmental policy, overseas Tamil welfare, and philosophical thought.
promise_text_ta: கன்னியாகுமரி, மாநிலத்தின் தொலைநோக்குச் சிந்தனை மற்றும் இயற்கை வளப் பாதுகாப்பிற்கான மையமாக, தமிழர் மெய்யியல் தலைநகரமாக (Epistemological Capital) இருக்கும். இது சுற்றுச்சூழல், வெளிநாடு வாழ் தமிழர் நலன் மற்றும் தத்துவ மையமாக விளங்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 35
impact_mechanism: This promise proposes creating a new center for long-term strategic thinking on policy, environment, and diaspora relations.

promise_components.component: Establish Kanyakumari as the Epistemological Capital for long-term vision, environmental policy, and overseas Tamil welfare.
promise_components.analysis: This is an abstract concept suggesting the creation of new institutions or think tanks. The cost is entirely dependent on the scale and nature of these new bodies, for which no data is available.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The cost of establishing new policy and research institutions is not specified.
sustainability_reasoning: The term 'Epistemological Capital' is novel but lacks a clear, deliverable mechanism. It serves more as a statement of philosophical intent for a thoughtful style of governance rather than a concrete, measurable plan.

promise_text_en: We will fight to secure separate, reserved constituencies for women in the State Assembly and Parliament, similar to the existing reservations for Scheduled Castes, to ensure equitable representation for women from all communities.
promise_text_ta: பெண்களுக்குச் சட்டமன்றத்திலும், நாடாளுமன்றத்திலும் (பட்டியலினத்தவர்களுக்கு ஒதுக்கப்பட்டுள்ளதைப் போல்) தனித்தொகுதிகள் போராடிப் பெறப்படும். இதனால் அனைத்து சமூகப் பெண்களுக்கான நிகர்நிலைக் கிடைக்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 37
impact_mechanism: This promise seeks to increase female political representation by advocating for constitutionally mandated reserved seats in legislatures.

promise_components.component: Fight to secure separate, reserved constituencies for women in the State Assembly and Parliament.
promise_components.analysis: This is a policy advocacy promise requiring a Constitutional Amendment by the Union Government. The state government has no direct power to implement this. The Women's Reservation Bill has been passed but its implementation is contingent on a future census and delimitation.

fiscal_cost_note: ₹0. This is a policy advocacy position directed at the Union Government and requires a constitutional amendment. It has no direct fiscal cost to the state.
sustainability_reasoning: This addresses the fundamental democratic deficit of women's under-representation by creating a legal mandate. It is a long-term structural reform, though its implementation is entirely outside the state's control.
coverage_gap_note: While benefiting all women through representation, the direct beneficiaries are women who contest from these reserved seats.

promise_text_en: To ensure fair representation based on population, the ratio will be changed to one parliamentary constituency for every three assembly constituencies. Our government will collaborate with other states to urge the Union government to make this change.
promise_text_ta: மக்கள் தொகைக்கு ஏற்ப, மூன்று சட்டமன்ற தொகுதிகளுக்கு ஒரு நாடாளுமன்றத் தொகுதி என மாற்ற வேண்டும். இந்த மாற்றத்திற்காக நாம் தமிழர் அரசு மற்ற மாநிலங்களுடன் சேர்ந்து ஒன்றிய அரசை வலியுறுத்தும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 39
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to increase the state's political influence in the national parliament by changing the allocation formula for seats.

promise_components.component: Change the ratio to one parliamentary constituency for every three assembly constituencies.
promise_components.analysis: This is a policy advocacy promise. Tamil Nadu currently has a 6:1 ratio (234 Assembly to 39 Lok Sabha seats). A 3:1 ratio would imply 78 Lok Sabha seats, nearly doubling the state's representation. This requires a major constitutional amendment and a nationwide delimitation exercise, which the state cannot do alone.

fiscal_cost_note: ₹0. This is a demand for a constitutional amendment directed at the Union Government. It has no direct fiscal cost to the state.
sustainability_reasoning: This seeks to fundamentally alter the federal balance of power by changing how parliamentary seats are allocated. It addresses the structural issue of representation for states with lower population growth, but is dependent on national consensus and central government action.

promise_text_en: The President of India should be directly elected by the people. Our government will formally demand that the Union of India amend the Constitution to implement this electoral reform.
promise_text_ta: நாட்டின் குடியரசுத்தலைவரை மக்களே நேரடியாகத் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கும்படியாகத் தேர்தல் முறை இருக்க வேண்டும். அதற்கேற்றாற்போல் அரசியலமைப்புச் சட்டத்தை மாற்ற வேண்டும் என்ற கோரிக்கையை நாம் தமிழர் அரசு இந்திய ஒன்றியத்தின் முன் வைக்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 39
impact_mechanism: This promise advocates for a fundamental change in India's political system by making the election for the head of state a direct popular vote.

promise_components.component: Demand the Union of India amend the Constitution to implement the direct election of the President.
promise_components.analysis: This is a policy advocacy promise directed at the Union Government. It requires a constitutional amendment to change India's parliamentary system. The state government has no power to implement this.

fiscal_cost_note: ₹0. This is a demand for a constitutional amendment directed at the Union Government. It has no direct fiscal cost to the state.
sustainability_reasoning: This proposes a radical transformation of India's political system. Given the extreme difficulty and lack of national consensus for such a change, it functions as political signalling about the party's ideology rather than a deliverable promise.

promise_text_en: The practice of individuals who are not directly elected by the people becoming Union Ministers via the Rajya Sabha is against democratic principles. Our party will fight for constitutional amendments to end this.
promise_text_ta: மக்களால் நேரடியாகத் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்படாதவர்கள் மாநிலங்களவை உறுப்பினர்களாகி ஒன்றிய அமைச்சர் பதவிகளுக்கு வருவது மக்களாட்சி மாண்பிற்கு எதிரானது. இது சார்ந்த சட்டத் திருத்தத்தைக் கொண்டுவர நாம் தமிழர் கட்சி போராடும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 39
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to strengthen democratic accountability by ensuring only directly elected representatives can become Union Ministers.

promise_components.component: Fight for constitutional amendments to end the practice of Rajya Sabha members becoming Union Ministers.
promise_components.analysis: This is a policy advocacy promise directed at the Union Government, seeking to amend Article 75 of the Constitution. The state government has no power to implement this. It would restrict the Prime Minister's ability to appoint experts or political figures who are not in the Lok Sabha.

fiscal_cost_note: ₹0. This is a demand for a constitutional amendment directed at the Union Government. It has no direct fiscal cost to the state.
sustainability_reasoning: This targets a specific feature of the parliamentary system that allows non-directly elected individuals to hold executive power. It is a structural reform aimed at enhancing direct accountability, though its implementation is beyond the state's purview.

promise_text_en: Transparency will be ensured in the appointment of the Information Commissioner, and the commission's functions will be regulated. Furthermore, action will be taken to resolve appeal applications within a 2-month period.
promise_text_ta: தகவல் ஆணையர் பணியமர்த்தத்தில் வெளிப்படைத்தன்மையை உறுதிசெய்து, ஆணையத்தின் செயல்பாடுகள் முறைப்படுத்தப்படும். மேலும், மேல்முறையீட்டு விண்ணப்பங்களை 2 மாதக் காலத்திற்குள் முடித்திட நடவடிக்கை எடுக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 41
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to improve government accountability and responsiveness by reforming the state's Right to Information (RTI) machinery.

promise_components.component: Ensure transparency in the appointment of the Information Commissioner.
promise_components.analysis: This is a procedural governance reform. Its impact cannot be quantified with the available reference data.

promise_components.component: Regulate the Information Commission's functions.
promise_components.analysis: This is a procedural governance reform. Its impact cannot be quantified with the available reference data.

promise_components.component: Resolve appeal applications within a 2-month period.
promise_components.analysis: This sets a specific performance target for the Information Commission. Its feasibility depends on existing backlogs and administrative capacity, for which data is unavailable.

fiscal_cost_note: This is a procedural and administrative reform with no significant direct fiscal cost. The cost is uncosted as it primarily involves changes to rules and processes, not new expenditure programs.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise targets the institutional framework of transparency and accountability. By setting clear timelines and reforming appointment processes, it aims to fix systemic weaknesses in the RTI process, creating a lasting change in governance.

promise_text_en: We will urge the Union Government to withdraw the amendment made to Section 8(1)(j) of the RTI Act under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act. The transparency of the Right to Information Act will be protected.
promise_text_ta: தனிப்பட்டவர் தரவுப் பாதுகாப்பு (DPDP) சட்டத்தின்கீழ், RTI பிரிவு 8(1)(j)இல் கொண்டுவரப்பட்ட திருத்தத்தைத் திரும்பப் பெற ஒன்றிய அரசிடம் வலியுறுத்தப்படும். தகவல் அறியும் உரிமைச் சட்டத்தின் வெளிப்படைத்தன்மை பாதுகாக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 41
impact_mechanism: This promise seeks to protect citizens' right to information by advocating against a central government amendment that weakens the RTI Act.

promise_components.component: Urge the Union Government to withdraw the amendment made to Section 8(1)(j) of the RTI Act under the DPDP Act.
promise_components.analysis: This is an advocacy promise directed at the central government. The state government has no direct authority to implement this change. Its success depends entirely on the Union Government's response.

fiscal_cost_note: There is no direct fiscal cost to the state budget for this advocacy-based promise. It is uncosted.
sustainability_reasoning: While the issue is important for transparency, the promise is limited to 'urging' the Union Government. The state government lacks the authority to enact this change, making it a political statement rather than a deliverable policy.

promise_text_en: As per the 74th Constitutional Amendment, power will be decentralized to the ward level, and urban local bodies will be transformed into self-governing units. Comprehensive amendments will be made to the Tamil Nadu Local Bodies Acts regarding this.
promise_text_ta: 74ஆவது அரசமைப்புச் சட்டத் திருத்தத்தின்படி, வட்ட (Ward) அளவில் அதிகாரத்தைப் பரவலாக்கி, நகர்ப்புற உள்ளாட்சி அமைப்புகள் தன்னாட்சி அதிகாரம் கொண்ட பிரிவுகளாக மாற்றப்படும். இதுகுறித்துத் தமிழ்நாடு உள்ளாட்சிச் சட்டங்களில் விரிவாகத் திருத்தம் மேற்கொள்ளப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 41
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to deepen local democracy by devolving power and functions to urban local bodies, making them more autonomous.

promise_components.component: Decentralize power to the ward level and transform urban local bodies into self-governing units as per the 74th Constitutional Amendment.
promise_components.analysis: This is a major governance restructuring involving legislative amendments to the Tamil Nadu Local Bodies Acts. The impact cannot be numerically quantified from the provided data.

fiscal_cost_note: This is a governance reform involving devolution of funds and functions, not a new spending scheme. The net fiscal impact is uncosted as it represents a restructuring of public finances rather than a direct new expenditure.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise addresses the root cause of weak local governance by proposing legal changes to empower local bodies. If implemented, it would create a lasting structural shift in the state's governance framework, aligning it with the 74th Amendment's intent.
coverage_gap_note: This promise specifically targets urban local bodies, so rural areas are not covered by this particular reform.

promise_text_en: Similar to Meghalaya, a 'Social Audit Act' will be enacted in Tamil Nadu to monitor government schemes, redress grievances, and audit works.
promise_text_ta: மேகாலயா மாநிலத்தைப் போல, அரசுத் திட்டங்களைக் கண்காணிக்கவும், குறைகளைத் தீர்க்கவும், பணிகளைத் தணிக்கை செய்யவும் தமிழகத்திலும் 'சமூகத் தணிக்கைச் சட்டம்' (Social Audit Act) இயற்றப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 41
impact_mechanism: This promise intends to increase public oversight and accountability of government schemes by creating a new legal framework for social audits.

promise_components.component: Enact a 'Social Audit Act' to monitor government schemes, redress grievances, and audit works.
promise_components.analysis: This involves creating new legislation modeled on Meghalaya's law. It would establish a formal, citizen-led mechanism for accountability. The reference data does not allow for a quantitative analysis of its impact.

fiscal_cost_note: The promise is uncosted. While enacting the law itself is not expensive, establishing and running a Social Audit Unit and facilitating audits across the state would require a dedicated budget, the size of which cannot be estimated from available data.
sustainability_reasoning: By creating a permanent legal framework for social audits, this promise addresses the systemic problem of inadequate public oversight in scheme implementation. It institutionalizes citizen participation, making it a structural reform for transparency.

promise_text_en: The misuse of Section 41 of the Chennai City Police Act and BNSS 163 (formerly CrPC 144) to suppress the democratic right to peaceful protest will be stopped immediately.
promise_text_ta: சென்னை மாநகரக் காவல் சட்டப் பிரிவு 41 மற்றும் BNSS 163 (முன்பு CrPC 144) ஆகியவற்றைத் தவறாகப் பயன்படுத்தி, அமைதி வழியில் போராடும் மக்களாட்சி உரிமையைத் தடுப்பது உடனடியாக நிறுத்தப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 41
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to protect the democratic right to peaceful assembly by changing the state's policy on the use of specific legal provisions.

promise_components.component: Stop the misuse of Section 41 of the Chennai City Police Act and BNSS 163 (formerly CrPC 144) to suppress peaceful protest.
promise_components.analysis: This is a policy directive to law enforcement agencies. It does not require legislative change but a shift in executive practice. Its impact is on civil liberties and cannot be quantified financially.

fiscal_cost_note: This is a policy change regarding the application of existing laws and has no direct fiscal cost. It is uncosted.
sustainability_reasoning: This addresses the alleged misuse of state power, a root cause of shrinking democratic space. By changing the state's enforcement policy, it alters the structural relationship between citizens and law enforcement regarding the right to protest.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar Katchi insists on the demand to amend the Constitution of the Indian Union to suit the current political, social, and global order, taking into account the rights of the existing national ethnicities.
promise_text_ta: இந்திய ஒன்றிய அரசியலைப்புச் சட்டத்தைத் தற்கால அரசியல், சமூக, சூழல்களுக்கு உலக ஒழுங்கிற்கு ஏற்றாற்போல மாற்றி அமைக்க வேண்டும் என்ற கோரிக்கையை நாம் தமிழர் கட்சி வலியுறுத்துகின்றது.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 41
impact_mechanism: This promise is an advocacy position calling for a fundamental rewrite of the Indian Constitution to better reflect ethnic rights.

promise_components.component: Insist on the demand to amend the Constitution of the Indian Union to suit the current political, social, and global order, taking into account the rights of the existing national ethnicities.
promise_components.analysis: This is an advocacy promise directed at the Union Government. A state government has no authority to amend the Indian Constitution. This is a statement of political ideology.

fiscal_cost_note: There is no fiscal cost to the state for making a demand for constitutional amendment. The promise is uncosted.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a political statement about national-level constitutional reform, which a state government cannot implement. It serves as political signalling to a specific ideological base rather than a deliverable policy.

promise_text_en: Under Naam Tamilar rule, with the slogan of 'Federalism at the Centre, Autonomy in the State', we will strive to bring about appropriate changes in the Indian Constitution so that the state government has the authority to legislate on all subjects.
promise_text_ta: நாம் தமிழர் ஆட்சியில் ஒன்றியத்தில் கூட்டாட்சி, மாநிலத்தில் தன்னாட்சி என்ற முழக்கத்தோடு அனைத்து வகையான துறைகளுக்கும் சட்டமியற்றும் அதிகாரம் மாநில அரசிற்கு உண்டு என்கிற வகையில் இந்திய ஒன்றிய அரசியலைப்புச் சட்டத்தில் உரிய மாற்றம் கொண்டு வரப் பாடுபடுவோம்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 43
impact_mechanism: This promise advocates for a radical restructuring of Indian federalism to grant the state full legislative autonomy.

promise_components.component: Strive to bring about appropriate changes in the Indian Constitution so that the state government has the authority to legislate on all subjects.
promise_components.analysis: This requires a constitutional amendment to abolish the Union and Concurrent lists of the Seventh Schedule. A state government cannot enact this; it can only advocate for it. This is a core ideological demand.

fiscal_cost_note: There is no fiscal cost to the state for advocating for constitutional change. The promise is uncosted.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise proposes a fundamental change to the Constitution of India, which is far beyond the powers of a state government. It functions as a statement of political ideology and is not a practically deliverable state-level policy.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar government will take over the administration of railways operating within the state's borders through a constitutional amendment.
promise_text_ta: மாநில எல்லைக்குள் பயணிக்கும் தொடர் வண்டி நிருவாகத் துறையினை நாம் தமிழர் அரசு இந்திய அரசியலைப்புச் சட்டமாற்றம் மூலம் கையகப்படுத்தும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 43
impact_mechanism: This promise advocates for state control over a major piece of central infrastructure, the railways, through constitutional change.

promise_components.component: Take over the administration of railways operating within the state's borders through a constitutional amendment.
promise_components.analysis: Railways are a Union List subject. This promise requires a constitutional amendment, which a state government cannot enact. It is an advocacy position for a transfer of power from the Union to the State.

fiscal_cost_note: The promise is uncosted. While the advocacy itself has no cost, the actual administration of railways would entail enormous financial liability, which is not part of the state's current budget structure.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a statement of political ambition that is constitutionally undeliverable by a state government. It serves to signal a strong stance on state autonomy rather than being a feasible policy proposal.

promise_text_en: We will take appropriate measures to bring about legal changes that reduce judicial overreach and give more value to public sentiment and democracy.
promise_text_ta: நீதிமன்ற மேலாண்மையைக் குறைத்து, மக்கள் உணர்வுகளுக்கு, மக்களாட்சிக்கு மதிப்பளிக்கக்கூடிய, உரிய சட்ட மாற்றங்களைக் கொண்டுவரத் தகுந்த முயற்சிகளை முன்னெடுப்போம்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 43
impact_mechanism: This promise expresses an intent to curb the power of the judiciary, framing it as a move to prioritize public sentiment.

promise_components.component: Take appropriate measures to bring about legal changes that reduce judicial overreach.
promise_components.analysis: This is a vague promise that challenges the principle of separation of powers. The executive/legislature has very limited constitutional means to 'reduce judicial overreach'. The mechanism for implementation is unclear.

fiscal_cost_note: There is no direct fiscal cost associated with this vaguely worded promise. It is uncosted.
sustainability_reasoning: The promise is a political statement against the judiciary with no clear or constitutional path to implementation. It reflects a political position rather than a concrete, deliverable governance reform.

promise_text_en: We will insist on making appropriate changes to the Indian Constitution so that the High Courts of the respective states can function as the highest appellate courts with supreme authority for cases within that state.
promise_text_ta: அந்தந்த மாநிலங்களின் தலைமை நீதிமன்றங்களே உச்ச அதிகாரம் பெற்ற நீதிமன்றங்களாகச் செயல்பட்டு தீர்ப்பினை வழங்கிட இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பில் உரிய மாற்றத்தினை ஏற்படுத்த வலியுறுத்தப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 45
impact_mechanism: This promise advocates for a major judicial reform to make state High Courts the final courts of appeal for state-level cases.

promise_components.component: Insist on making appropriate changes to the Indian Constitution so that the High Courts of the respective states can function as the highest appellate courts for cases within that state.
promise_components.analysis: This requires a constitutional amendment to curtail the appellate jurisdiction of the Supreme Court. A state government can only advocate for such a change; it cannot implement it.

fiscal_cost_note: There is no fiscal cost to the state for advocating for constitutional change. The promise is uncosted.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a call for a fundamental restructuring of the national judiciary, a power the state government does not possess. The promise is a form of political signalling about judicial federalism, not a deliverable state policy.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar government will join with other states to raise its voice and strive to bring about a 'legal change' so that natives of that land are appointed as the Chief Justice of their respective state High Courts.
promise_text_ta: அந்தந்த மாநில அரசின் தலைமை நீதிபதியாக, அந்தந்த மண்ணின் மைந்தர்களே அமர்த்தப்படும் வகையில் ‘சட்ட மாற்றத்தினை’ ஏற்படுத்த நாம் தமிழர் அரசு, மற்ற மாநிலங்களோடு இணைந்து குரலெழுப்பும், பாடுபடும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 45
impact_mechanism: Advocates for a constitutional/legal change to mandate the appointment of native judges as Chief Justices of state High Courts.

promise_components.component: Join with other states to bring about a 'legal change' so that natives of that land are appointed as the Chief Justice of their respective state High Courts.
promise_components.analysis: The appointment of High Court judges, including the Chief Justice, is governed by Article 217 of the Constitution and is made by the President of India in consultation with the Chief Justice of India and the Governor of the State. A state government has no direct power to implement this and can only advocate for such a change.

fiscal_cost_note: No direct fiscal cost to the state. This is a political advocacy position, not a state-funded program.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise is a political statement on regional representation within the federal structure. The state government lacks the constitutional authority to implement it, making it a matter of political signalling rather than a deliverable policy.
coverage_gap_note: This policy would benefit only a very small number of senior judges eligible for the post of Chief Justice.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar government will review the current reservation system. It will conduct a proper caste-wise census and ensure reservation is provided accordingly.
promise_text_ta: தற்போது நடைமுறையில் உள்ள இடப்பங்கீட்டு முறையை நாம் தமிழர் அரசு சீராய்வு செய்யும். முறையாக சாதிவாரி மக்கள் தொகைக் கணக்கெடுப்பை மேற்கொண்டு, அதன்படி இடப்பங்கீட்டினை உறுதி செய்யும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 45
impact_mechanism: Aims to realign the state's affirmative action policies by conducting a caste-wise census and adjusting reservation quotas accordingly.

promise_components.component: Conduct a proper caste-wise census.
promise_components.analysis: The power to conduct a census is vested with the Union Government under the Census Act, 1948. While a state can conduct its own socio-economic survey, it is a massive and logistically complex undertaking. The cost for such a survey is not available in the reference data.

promise_components.component: Ensure reservation is provided according to the census.
promise_components.analysis: Altering reservation quotas based on new data requires legislative action. This would be subject to legal scrutiny, particularly concerning the 50% reservation cap established by the Supreme Court, although Tamil Nadu's 69% reservation has special protection under the 9th Schedule of the Constitution.

fiscal_cost_note: The cost of conducting a state-wide caste census would be substantial, but specific figures are unavailable. The policy change itself is a reallocation of opportunities, not a direct expenditure.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise targets the foundational data upon which affirmative action is based. If implemented, it would structurally alter the distribution of educational and employment opportunities to align with contemporary demographics, addressing the root issue of equitable representation.

promise_text_en: Priority will be given to Tamil people. Whatever priority is given to the Tamil people in other linguistic states, the same priority will be given to people of other linguistic nationalities in Tamil Nadu.
promise_text_ta: தமிழ்க்குடிகளுக்கு முன்னுரிமை வழங்கி, பிறமொழிவழி மாநிலங்களில் தமிழின மக்களுக்கு என்ன முன்னுரிமை வழங்கப்படுகிறதோ, அதே முன்னுரிமை பிறமொழிவழித் தேசிய இன மக்களுக்குத் தமிழ்நாட்டில் வழங்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 45
impact_mechanism: Proposes a reciprocal policy of giving preference to Tamil people in Tamil Nadu, mirroring the treatment of Tamils in other states.

promise_components.component: Priority will be given to Tamil people, with the level of priority matching that given to Tamils in other states.
promise_components.analysis: The term 'priority' is not defined. Implementing such a policy, especially in public employment, would likely violate Article 16 of the Constitution, which prohibits discrimination based on place of birth or residence. Exceptions require an act of Parliament, not state legislation.

fiscal_cost_note: No direct fiscal cost. However, it carries a high risk of legal challenges and potential negative economic consequences on labour mobility.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a political statement on linguistic nationalism that is likely unconstitutional. Its practical implementation is blocked by fundamental rights, making it a form of political signalling rather than a viable policy.
coverage_gap_note: This policy could negatively impact non-Tamil residents of Tamil Nadu.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar government will fight alongside other state governments for a system where 75% of the taxes collected in a state go to the state government and 25% to the Union government.
promise_text_ta: அந்தந்த மாநிலத்தில் பெறப்படும் வரிகளில் 75 விழுக்காடு மாநில அரசுக்கும், 25 விழுக்காடு ஒன்றிய அரசுக்குமாகப் பிரித்துக் கொள்ளும் வகையில் மற்ற மாநில அரசுகளுடன் இணைந்து நாம் தமிழர் அரசு போராடும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 45
impact_mechanism: Advocates for a fundamental shift in fiscal federalism, demanding a greater share of tax revenue for the state.

promise_components.component: Fight for a system where 75% of taxes collected in a state go to the state and 25% to the Union.
promise_components.analysis: The distribution of taxes between the Union and states is governed by the Constitution and the recommendations of the Finance Commission. A state government cannot unilaterally change this formula; it can only lobby the Union government and other states for a constitutional amendment.

fiscal_cost_note: This is not a cost but a proposed revenue model change. It is not within the state's power to implement.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise addresses the root cause of state fiscal dependency by proposing a fundamental restructuring of fiscal federalism. While its achievement is outside the state's direct control, the goal itself is structural in nature, aiming for greater state autonomy.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar government will immediately take appropriate measures to make the mother tongue Tamil the official administrative language in all courts, from the High Court to the local courts.
promise_text_ta: உயர்நீதிமன்றம் முதல் உள்ளூர் நீதிமன்றம் வரை அனைத்து நீதிமன்றங்களிலும் தாய்மொழித் தமிழை ஆட்சிமொழியாக அலுவல் மொழியாக ஏற்படுத்த உரிய நடவடிக்கைகளை நாம் தமிழர் அரசு உடனடியாக எடுக்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 46
impact_mechanism: Seeks to improve access to justice for Tamil speakers by making Tamil the official language of the state's judiciary.

promise_components.component: Make Tamil the official administrative language in all courts, from the High Court to local courts.
promise_components.analysis: For subordinate courts, this is within the state's purview in coordination with the High Court. For the High Court, Article 348(2) of the Constitution allows the use of a state's official language with the prior consent of the President of India, a consent which has historically been difficult to obtain.

fiscal_cost_note: The fiscal cost would involve administrative expenses for translation, training, and system updates. A precise figure is unavailable, but it is expected to be manageable within the state budget.
sustainability_reasoning: This addresses a key structural barrier in the justice system: language. By enabling proceedings in the local language, it makes the legal process more accessible and understandable for the majority of the population, tackling a root cause of inequitable access to justice.
coverage_gap_note: This may pose challenges for legal practitioners and judges not proficient in Tamil.

promise_text_en: Everyone who has the right to vote must vote. The aim of the Naam Tamilar government is to ensure 100% voter turnout by making it a mandatory duty.
promise_text_ta: வாக்குரிமை பெற்ற எல்லோரும் கட்டாயம் வாக்களிக்க வேண்டும். நூறு விழுக்காடு வாக்குப்பதிவை உறுதி செய்வதே நாம் தமிழர் அரசின் நோக்கம்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 46
impact_mechanism: Aims to increase democratic participation by making voting a mandatory civic duty.

promise_components.component: Ensure 100% voter turnout by making voting a mandatory duty.
promise_components.analysis: Making voting compulsory requires an amendment to the Representation of the People Act, a central legislation. A state government does not have the legislative competence to enforce this. It can only conduct awareness campaigns.

fiscal_cost_note: No direct fiscal cost for the mandatory voting aspect, as it is outside the state's jurisdiction. Awareness campaigns would have a manageable cost.
sustainability_reasoning: The core of the promise—making voting mandatory—is legally unenforceable by a state government. Therefore, it functions as a political statement about democratic participation rather than a deliverable policy.

promise_text_en: The Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) system will be removed and the ballot paper system will be implemented. Ballot papers will be printed with a counterfoil, and the receipt portion will be torn off and given to the voter.
promise_text_ta: மின்னணு வாக்குப்பதிவு முறை நீக்கப்பட்டு, வாக்குச் சீட்டு முறை நடைமுறைப்படுத்தப்படும். அடிக்கட்டை நுனிக்கட்டையோடு வாக்குச் சீட்டு அச்சடிக்கப்படும். வாக்குச் செலுத்தியவருக்கு அதற்கான ஏற்பு பகுதி கிழித்து வழங்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 46
impact_mechanism: Proposes to change the method of voting from electronic machines to paper ballots to address concerns about electoral integrity.

promise_components.component: The Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) system will be removed and the ballot paper system will be implemented.
promise_components.analysis: The conduct of elections and the choice of voting technology (EVMs vs. ballot papers) are under the exclusive jurisdiction of the Election Commission of India (ECI) and governed by central laws. A state government has no authority to change the voting system for state or national elections.

fiscal_cost_note: Not applicable, as the state government cannot implement this policy. The cost would be borne by the ECI.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise addresses a political debate about EVM credibility but is entirely outside the state's power to implement. It is a statement of political position directed at a central constitutional body, not a feasible state-level action.

promise_text_en: To prevent fraudulent voting, our government will urge the Election Commission that a certificate must be issued to the voter after voting.
promise_text_ta: கள்ள வாக்குப் பதிவினைத் தடுக்க, வாக்களித்த பிறகு வாக்காளருக்குச் சான்று வழங்க வேண்டும் என நமது அரசு தேர்தல் ஆணையத்திடம் வலியுறுத்தும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 46
impact_mechanism: Advocates for a procedural change in voting to enhance transparency and prevent fraudulent voting.

promise_components.component: Urge the Election Commission that a certificate must be issued to the voter after voting.
promise_components.analysis: This is a suggestion to the Election Commission of India (ECI). The current VVPAT system allows a voter to see a paper slip confirming their vote, but it is not handed over. The state government can only 'urge' the ECI to adopt this change; it cannot mandate it.

fiscal_cost_note: No cost to the state budget. This is an advocacy effort directed at the ECI.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a minor procedural suggestion to a central body, the ECI. As the state's role is limited to advocacy, and a similar mechanism (VVPAT) already exists, the promise has minimal new material benefit and serves primarily as political signalling.

promise_text_en: The right to vote by post will be secured for all Tamil people living abroad for their livelihood.
promise_text_ta: வாழ்வாதாரத்திற்காக வெளிநாட்டில் வாழ்கின்ற அனைத்துத் தமிழ் மக்களுக்கும் அஞ்சல் வழியில் வாக்களிக்கும் உரிமை பெற்றுத் தரப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 47
impact_mechanism: Aims to enfranchise citizens living abroad by securing for them the right to vote via postal ballot.

promise_components.component: The right to vote by post will be secured for all Tamil people living abroad for their livelihood.
promise_components.analysis: Voting rights for Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) are governed by the Representation of the People Act, a central law. Currently, NRIs can only vote in person. Extending postal voting would require an amendment by the Parliament of India. The state government's role is limited to advocacy.

fiscal_cost_note: No cost to the state budget. The implementation and costs would be managed by the Election Commission of India.
sustainability_reasoning: While this addresses a genuine root cause of disenfranchisement for NRIs, the state government has no power to implement it. The promise is therefore 'optics' as it is an advocacy position on a matter of central jurisdiction, not a deliverable state policy.

promise_text_en: Appropriate steps will be taken to establish a bench of the Supreme Court in Chennai. A civil and criminal court with the authority of a district court will be established in each legislative assembly constituency. Vacant judicial positions will be filled based on social justice principles.
promise_text_ta: தமிழகத்தில் உச்சநீதிமன்றக் கிளையைச் சென்னையில் அமைக்க உரிய நடவடிக்கை எடுக்கப்படும். ஒவ்வொரு சட்டமன்றத் தொகுதியிலும் மாவட்ட நீதிமன்ற அதிகாரம் கொண்ட உரிமையியல் குற்றவியல் நீதிமன்றம் அமைக்கப்படும். காலியாக உள்ள நீதிபதிகள் பணியிடம் சமூகநீதிக் கொள்கைகள் அடிப்படையில் நிரப்பப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 47
impact_mechanism: Aims to improve access to justice by expanding judicial infrastructure at both the highest and local levels.

promise_components.component: Take appropriate steps to establish a bench of the Supreme Court in Chennai.
promise_components.analysis: Establishing a Supreme Court bench outside Delhi requires a decision by the Chief Justice of India with the President's approval (Article 130). A state government can only advocate for this.

promise_components.component: A civil and criminal court with the authority of a district court will be established in each legislative assembly constituency.
promise_components.analysis: Establishing subordinate courts is a state function, done in consultation with the High Court. Creating courts in all 234 assembly constituencies would be a massive infrastructure project. Cost data is unavailable.

promise_components.component: Vacant judicial positions will be filled based on social justice principles.
promise_components.analysis: The state government, in coordination with the High Court and state public service commission, can expedite the filling of vacancies in the subordinate judiciary, following existing reservation policies.

fiscal_cost_note: Establishing courts in 234 constituencies would entail massive capital and recurring expenditure, likely straining the state's discretionary budget. Cost is 'data unavailable — cannot calculate'. Filling vacancies is a budgeted cost.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise addresses root causes of judicial backlog and inaccessibility: geographic distance and judicial vacancies. Expanding the court network and ensuring full staffing are structural changes that create lasting improvements in the justice delivery system.
coverage_gap_note: A court per constituency may not be the most efficient allocation; some urban areas may be over-served while large rural areas remain under-served.

promise_text_en: An Official Language Act will be brought in to ensure all forms, from the lower courts to the High Court, are in Tamil.
promise_text_ta: கீழ் நீதிமன்றம் முதல் உயர் நீதிமன்றம் வரையிலும் அனைத்துப் படிவங்களும் தமிழில் அமைய ஆட்சி மொழிச் சட்டம் கொண்டு வரப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 47
impact_mechanism: This aims to improve access to justice for Tamil-speaking citizens by enabling them to use their native language in all court proceedings.

promise_components.component: An Official Language Act will be brought in to ensure all forms, from the lower courts to the High Court, are in Tamil.
promise_components.analysis: This is a legislative and administrative action. Using Tamil in the High Court is governed by Article 348 of the Constitution and requires Presidential assent. The direct fiscal cost involves translation, reprinting forms, and staff training, which cannot be quantified with the available data.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The cost is administrative and not possible to estimate from the provided reference data.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a one-time legislative change that permanently alters the language of legal proceedings, addressing a fundamental barrier to access to justice for Tamil speakers. It is a systemic reform, not a recurring subsidy.
coverage_gap_note: Non-Tamil speaking litigants and lawyers within the state might face new language barriers.

promise_text_en: Legal aid centers will be established from village panchayats to municipal corporations to provide legal assistance. Young law graduates will be engaged in these services, and this social service will be made mandatory for all young lawyers upon graduation.
promise_text_ta: சிற்றூராட்சி முதல் மாநகராட்சி வரை சட்டம் தொடர்பான உதவிகள் செய்வதற்குச் சட்ட உதவி மையம் அமைக்கப்படும். இச்சேவைகளில் சட்டக்கல்வி முடித்த இளம் வழக்கறிஞர்கள் ஈடுபடுத்தப்படுவார்கள். இந்தச் சமூகச் சேவை சட்டக்கல்வி முடிக்கின்ற இளம் வழக்கறிஞர்கள் அனைவருக்கும் கட்டாயமாக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 47
impact_mechanism: This expands access to legal aid for citizens by creating a new delivery infrastructure at the local level, staffed by new law graduates.

promise_components.component: Legal aid centers will be established from village panchayats to municipal corporations.
promise_components.analysis: The number of village panchayats and municipal corporations is not available in the reference data, so the number of centers and associated capital/operational costs cannot be calculated.

promise_components.component: Young law graduates will be engaged in these services, and this social service will be made mandatory for all young lawyers upon graduation.
promise_components.analysis: This is a regulatory change for the legal profession. The number of law graduates per year is not available, so the cost of stipends or salaries cannot be calculated. Making service mandatory requires significant legislative action and could face legal challenges.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The number of centers, required staff, and their remuneration are not defined in the promise or available in the reference data.
sustainability_reasoning: By establishing permanent centers and a mandatory service pipeline, this promise creates a lasting system to address the lack of affordable legal aid. It's not a temporary fix but a new institutional framework.
coverage_gap_note: The quality of legal aid may be inconsistent if it relies on mandatory service from potentially unwilling recent graduates.

promise_text_en: Internet facilities will be immediately provided in all courts across the state. All parties involved in a case will be notified of hearing dates via SMS on their mobile phones.
promise_text_ta: மாநிலம் முழுக்க இருக்கின்ற நீதிமன்றங்களில் இணையத்தள வசதி உடனடியாக ஏற்படுத்தப்படும். வழக்கின் வினாவுதல் நாள்கள் வழக்கில் தொடர்புடைய அனைவருக்கும் கைப்பேசிக் குறுஞ்செய்தி மூலமாகத் தெரிவிக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 47
impact_mechanism: This improves judicial efficiency and transparency by using digital tools for court connectivity and case communication.

promise_components.component: Internet facilities will be immediately provided in all courts across the state.
promise_components.analysis: The total number of courts in Tamil Nadu is not available in the reference data, so the cost of hardware, installation, and recurring internet subscriptions cannot be calculated.

promise_components.component: All parties involved in a case will be notified of hearing dates via SMS on their mobile phones.
promise_components.analysis: This requires a software system and a bulk SMS service. The number of cases or litigants is not in the reference data, so the operational cost cannot be calculated.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The number of courts and volume of SMS notifications are unknown. However, such e-governance initiatives are typically low-cost relative to major welfare schemes.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a modernization effort that upgrades the court system's infrastructure. Once implemented, the digital communication channels become a permanent feature, improving efficiency without recurring large-scale expenditure.
coverage_gap_note: Individuals without a mobile phone or in areas with poor network coverage would not receive SMS notifications.

promise_text_en: Our governance will be a 'selfless, benevolent dictatorship'.
promise_text_ta: நமது ஆட்சிமுறை ‘தன்னலமற்ற அன்பான சர்வதிகார ஆட்சிமுறை’.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 49
impact_mechanism: This is a statement of governing philosophy, not a specific, measurable policy action with a tangible impact mechanism.

promise_components.component: Our governance will be a 'selfless, benevolent dictatorship'.
promise_components.analysis: This is a political slogan and cannot be decomposed into a measurable commitment. It is contrary to the democratic principles of the Indian Constitution and has no analyzable policy components.

fiscal_cost_note: Not applicable. This is a statement of intent, not a fiscal promise.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a purely rhetorical statement about a style of governance. It has no material benefit, no implementation plan, and is fundamentally at odds with the democratic and constitutional structure of the state.

promise_text_en: The commercial sale of water will be banned.
promise_text_ta: அதனால் தான் ‘தண்ணீர் விற்பனை’ தடை என்கிறோம்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 55
impact_mechanism: This aims to conserve groundwater and assert public ownership over water by prohibiting its treatment as a commercial product.

promise_components.component: The commercial sale of water will be banned.
promise_components.analysis: This is a major regulatory change affecting the packaged water industry and private tanker suppliers. The economic impact, potential loss of tax revenue, and enforcement costs cannot be calculated from the reference data.

fiscal_cost_note: Not a direct expenditure. The fiscal impact would be indirect, through loss of tax revenue from banned industries and enforcement costs. The magnitude is unknown.
sustainability_reasoning: A complete ban on the commercial sale of water is a radical, structural shift in resource management policy. It attempts to fix the problem of water commodification at its source, regardless of the practical challenges.
coverage_gap_note: Households and businesses in areas with inadequate public water supply, who rely on private water sellers, would be left without a legal water source.

promise_text_en: The Naam Tamilar government will save rainwater that otherwise flows into the sea and make it available for public use.
promise_text_ta: நாம் தமிழர் அரசு, சேமிக்கப்படாமல் கடலில் கலக்கும் மழை நீரைச் சேமித்து மக்கள் பயன்பாட்டிற்கு வழங்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 59
impact_mechanism: This aims to increase freshwater availability for public use by capturing rainwater runoff that would otherwise be lost to the sea.

promise_components.component: The Naam Tamilar government will save rainwater that otherwise flows into the sea and make it available for public use.
promise_components.analysis: This is a general statement of intent for water conservation, implying the construction of infrastructure like check dams, reservoirs, and recharge wells. The scale, location, and cost of such projects are not specified and cannot be calculated.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The cost is entirely dependent on the scale and type of unspecified infrastructure projects. Large water projects are typically very high-cost.
sustainability_reasoning: Creating physical infrastructure to capture and store rainwater is a classic structural solution to water scarcity. It provides a long-term, sustainable augmentation of water supply if designed and maintained correctly.
coverage_gap_note: Benefits would likely be concentrated in the specific regions where new water storage infrastructure is constructed.

promise_text_en: The upcoming Naam Tamilar government will immediately implement various action plans to protect water bodies, increase water resources, remove encroachments, and rejuvenate waterways.
promise_text_ta: நீர்நிலைகளைக் காத்திட, நீர் ஆதாரங்களைப் பெருக்கிட, வன்கவர்புகளை அகற்றி நீர்வழித் தடங்களைப் புதுப்பித்திடப் பல்வேறு செயல் திட்டங்களை, அமையவிருக்கும் நாம் தமிழர் அரசு உடனடியாக நிறைவேற்றும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 60
impact_mechanism: This aims to improve water quality and quantity by restoring and protecting existing water bodies through concrete actions like desilting and encroachment removal.

promise_components.component: Implement various action plans to protect water bodies, increase water resources, remove encroachments, and rejuvenate waterways.
promise_components.analysis: This is a statement of intent covering multiple activities like desilting, fencing, and enforcement. The scope, number of water bodies, and scale of work are not defined, so costs cannot be calculated.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The scope of 'various action plans' is undefined, making any cost estimation impossible.
sustainability_reasoning: Protecting and rejuvenating water bodies is a structural investment in natural infrastructure. It addresses the root causes of water degradation and provides lasting benefits for water security and flood mitigation.

promise_text_en: To save and protect our water resources, a new water policy will be formulated based on the economics of rainwater.
promise_text_ta: நமது நீர் வளத்தைச் சேமித்துப் பாதுகாக்கும் பொருட்டு, புதிய நீர்க் கொள்கை மழைநீர் மறை பொருளாதாரத்தை அடிப்படையாகக் கொண்டு வகுக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 60
impact_mechanism: This proposes a paradigm shift in water management by creating a new state policy centered on the principle of rainwater harvesting and utilization.

promise_components.component: A new water policy will be formulated based on the economics of rainwater.
promise_components.analysis: This is a high-level policy formulation promise. It has no direct fiscal cost other than the administrative effort to draft the policy. The implementation of the subsequent policy would have costs, but these are currently unknown.

fiscal_cost_note: Not applicable for the policy formulation itself. The implementation of the resulting policy would have fiscal costs, but those are unknown.
sustainability_reasoning: Formulating a new governing policy is the definition of a structural approach. It seeks to change the rules, incentives, and principles that guide all future actions in the water sector.

promise_text_en: A dual pipeline system will be implemented to supply water to homes.
promise_text_ta: வீடுகளுக்கு இரட்டைக் குழாய் நீர் வழங்கல்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 60
impact_mechanism: This aims to conserve high-quality treated water by supplying a separate stream of non-potable water for uses like toilet flushing and gardening.

promise_components.component: A dual pipeline system will be implemented to supply water to homes.
promise_components.analysis: This requires laying a second, parallel water distribution network. This is a massive infrastructure project, especially as a retrofit in existing urban areas. The cost per household is very high, but the number of households is not specified, so a total cost cannot be calculated.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. The cost of laying a parallel pipe network across cities and towns would be extremely high, likely running into tens of thousands of crores and far exceeding the state's discretionary budget.
sustainability_reasoning: A dual pipeline system is a piece of permanent infrastructure that structurally changes how water is used, promoting conservation. However, its immense cost and logistical complexity make it an extremely challenging structural change to implement.
coverage_gap_note: Rural areas and households without existing piped water connections would be excluded from this system.

promise_text_en: The mixing of sewage into lakes, ponds, rivers, and other water bodies will be completely stopped.
promise_text_ta: ஏரிகள், குளங்கள், ஆறுகள், நீர்நிலைகள் ஆகியவற்றில் கலக்கும் கழிவுநீர் முற்றிலுமாகத் தடுக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 61
impact_mechanism: This aims to improve public health and restore aquatic ecosystems by eliminating the discharge of untreated sewage into water bodies.

promise_components.component: The mixing of sewage into lakes, ponds, rivers, and other water bodies will be completely stopped.
promise_components.analysis: This requires achieving 100% coverage of households and industries with a sewage network and building sufficient sewage treatment plants (STPs). The current infrastructure gap and associated costs cannot be calculated from the reference data.

fiscal_cost_note: data unavailable — cannot calculate. Achieving 100% sewage collection and treatment is a massive, capital-intensive goal. The cost would be exceptionally high, far exceeding the state's discretionary budget and requiring significant central government funding.
sustainability_reasoning: Creating a comprehensive sewage collection and treatment system is a fundamental, structural solution to water pollution. It permanently alters how wastewater is managed, yielding long-term environmental and public health benefits.

promise_text_en: No new industries will be permitted on riverbanks. Licenses of existing factories that do not properly implement 'purification centers' or release waste into rivers will be immediately revoked.
promise_text_ta: ஆற்றங்கரையோரம் எந்தவித தொழிற்சாலைகளுக்கும் அனுமதி வழங்கப்படாது. ஏற்கனவே இருக்கும் தொழிற்சாலைகள், ‘தூய்மைப்படுத்தும் மையத்தை’ சரியான முறையில் செயல்படுத்தவில்லை என்றால், அவர்களின் உரிமம் திரும்பப் பெறப்படும். கழிவுகளை ஆற்றில் கலக்கவிடும் தொழிற்சாலைகளின் உரிமமும் உடனடியாக திரும்பப் பெறப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 61
impact_mechanism: Prevents future river pollution and penalizes existing industrial polluters to improve water quality.

promise_components.component: Prohibit new industries on riverbanks.
promise_components.analysis: This is a regulatory policy change. The cost is administrative, for enforcement and monitoring. No data available to quantify this cost.

promise_components.component: Revoke licenses of existing polluting factories without proper purification.
promise_components.analysis: This is a punitive enforcement action. Costs are administrative and legal. Implementation would face significant legal and political challenges. No data available to quantify the cost or economic impact.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The direct costs are administrative and part of departmental budgets. The economic impact of revoking licenses is not quantifiable with available data.
sustainability_reasoning: This promise targets the root cause of industrial river pollution by creating a permanent regulatory framework and enforcement mechanism. It aims to change industrial behavior for long-term environmental preservation.
coverage_gap_note: The promise does not address non-industrial sources of river pollution, such as municipal sewage or agricultural runoff.

promise_text_en: Using maps of the 17 river basins in Tamil Nadu, their paths, tributaries, and connected water bodies will be identified and maintained. Encroachments will be removed, and the natural extent of water bodies will be restored.
promise_text_ta: தமிழகத்திலுள்ள 17 ஆற்றுப்படுகைகளின் நீர்வழித்தட வரைபடத்தினைக் கொண்டு அவை கடந்து வரும் பாதைகள், அவற்றின் கிளை ஆறுகள், அவற்றோடு தொடர்பில் இருக்கும் நீர்நிலைகள் முதலியவை கண்டறியப்பட்டுப் பேணிக்காக்கப்படும். வன்கவர்புகள் இருக்கும் நிலையில் அவை அகற்றப்பட்டு, நீர் நிலைகளின் இயற்கைப் பரப்பு மீட்டெடுக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 61
impact_mechanism: Restores natural water flow, storage capacity, and floodplains by clearing obstructions and reclaiming encroached land.

promise_components.component: Identify and map the 17 river basins, their tributaries, and connected water bodies.
promise_components.analysis: A large-scale geospatial and administrative project. The cost is not quantifiable from the provided data.

promise_components.component: Remove encroachments and restore the natural extent of water bodies.
promise_components.analysis: A massive public works and enforcement action. The cost of removal, and potential rehabilitation of displaced persons, is substantial but not quantifiable with available data.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. This would be a multi-year, high-expenditure project drawing significantly from the state's discretionary budget. The total cost is impossible to estimate without a defined scope.
sustainability_reasoning: This addresses the fundamental degradation of hydrological systems caused by encroachment. Restoring the natural boundaries of water bodies is a permanent, structural solution to improve water retention and mitigate floods.
coverage_gap_note: The promise does not detail the process for rehabilitating or compensating individuals displaced by encroachment removal.

promise_text_en: A law will be enacted to provide severe imprisonment for those who encroach on water bodies.
promise_text_ta: நீர் நிலைகளை வன்கவர்பு செய்பவர்களுக்கு கடுமையான சிறைத்தண்டனை வழங்கும் வகையில் சட்டம் இயற்றப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 61
impact_mechanism: Deters future illegal occupation of water bodies through the threat of severe legal penalties.

promise_components.component: Enact a law providing severe imprisonment for those who encroach on water bodies.
promise_components.analysis: This is a legislative action. The direct cost of drafting and passing a law is negligible. The true cost lies in the enforcement through the police and judicial systems, which is not quantifiable.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The direct fiscal impact is minimal, relating to legislative and administrative processes. Enforcement costs are unknown.
sustainability_reasoning: Creating a strong legal deterrent is a structural change to the governance framework protecting natural resources. It aims to prevent a root cause of water body degradation, rather than just fixing the symptoms.
coverage_gap_note: This law would deter future encroachment but does not, by itself, address the removal of existing encroachments.

promise_text_en: Water bodies such as lakes, kanmais, and ponds will be desilted and their banks will be strengthened.
promise_text_ta: ஏரி, கண்மாய், குளங்கள் போன்ற நீர்நிலைகள் தூர்வாரப்பட்டு அதன் கரைகள் வலுப்படுத்தப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 61
impact_mechanism: Increases the water storage capacity of local water bodies and prevents erosion by removing accumulated silt and reinforcing banks.

promise_components.component: Desilt water bodies such as lakes, kanmais, and ponds.
promise_components.analysis: A large-scale public works program. The number of water bodies in Tamil Nadu is vast, and the cost per unit is not provided. The total cost is not quantifiable.

promise_components.component: Strengthen the banks of these water bodies.
promise_components.analysis: A civil engineering activity often done in conjunction with desilting. The cost depends on the length of banks and materials used, which is not specified.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The scope is undefined, but desilting thousands of water bodies would be a significant, multi-year expense from the discretionary budget.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a capital investment in restoring the functionality of existing water infrastructure. It addresses the root cause of reduced storage capacity (siltation) and provides a lasting physical improvement.
coverage_gap_note: The promise lacks a prioritization mechanism, which could lead to inequitable implementation across different regions.

promise_text_en: Through water resource management in water-scarce river basins, arrangements will be made to ensure water availability in all parts of the basin. Following the national water policy, structures will be built at the national, regional, and village levels to ensure a continuous water supply.
promise_text_ta: தண்ணீர் பற்றாக்குறை ஆற்றுப்படுகைகளில் நீர்வள மேலாண்மை மூலம் அப்பகுதியின் எப்பகுதியிலும் தண்ணீர் கிடைக்க ஏற்பாடு செய்யப்படும். தேசிய நீர்க்கொள்கைப்படி தேசிய வட்டார மற்றும் சிற்றூர் அளவிலும், தொடர்ந்து தண்ணீர் கிடைக்கும் வகையில் கட்டுமானங்கள் அமைக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 61
impact_mechanism: Improves water distribution and availability in arid regions through strategic infrastructure and better management systems.

promise_components.component: Implement water resource management to ensure water availability in water-scarce river basins.
promise_components.analysis: A high-level policy and management objective. Costs are administrative and related to planning.

promise_components.component: Build structures at national, regional, and village levels for continuous water supply.
promise_components.analysis: A capital-intensive infrastructure promise. The type, number, and scale of 'structures' are undefined, making cost estimation impossible.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The term 'structures' is too vague to estimate cost, which could range from minor to extremely large. The mention of 'national' level may imply dependence on central funds.
sustainability_reasoning: This aims to structurally re-engineer the water supply network to address regional imbalances, a root cause of water stress. It focuses on building permanent assets and management systems.
coverage_gap_note: The focus on water-scarce basins might divert resources and attention from the maintenance needs of water-sufficient areas.

promise_text_en: Laws and regulations for the proper use of water will be drafted and implemented.
promise_text_ta: தண்ணீரை முறையாகப் பயன்படுத்தத்தக்க சட்ட திட்டங்கள் தீட்டி நடைமுறைப்படுத்தப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 63
impact_mechanism: Establishes a legal and regulatory framework to govern water consumption, promoting conservation and efficiency.

promise_components.component: Draft and implement laws and regulations for the proper use of water.
promise_components.analysis: A legislative and administrative action. The direct cost is minimal. The cost of enforcement across agricultural, industrial, and domestic sectors is unknown.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The direct cost is negligible. The cost of creating a new enforcement and monitoring machinery is unknown but could be significant.
sustainability_reasoning: This addresses the root cause of water wastage and inefficient use by establishing permanent rules of governance. It moves from an ad-hoc system to a rule-based one for water allocation and use.
coverage_gap_note: The effectiveness of the law is entirely dependent on its specific provisions and the state's capacity for rigorous enforcement.

promise_text_en: Instead of lining canals with concrete, natural methods will be adopted to allow water seepage and recharge groundwater.
promise_text_ta: கால்வாய்களில் பைஞ்சுதைத் தளம் அமைப்பதை விடுத்து, இயற்கை முறையில் நீர் கசிவு மூலம் நிலத்தடி நீர் உயர்வதற்கு வழிவகை செய்யப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 63
impact_mechanism: Enhances groundwater recharge by changing canal construction methods to allow natural water seepage into aquifers.

promise_components.component: Adopt natural methods for lining canals instead of concrete.
promise_components.analysis: This is a change in technical specifications for public works. It applies to new and renovated canals. The cost difference compared to concrete lining is not specified.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. This is a change in methodology, and the net financial impact (material costs vs. water loss) is not quantifiable from the available data.
sustainability_reasoning: This is a structural change in infrastructure design that prioritizes ecological benefits (groundwater recharge) over pure engineering efficiency. It addresses the root cause of falling groundwater tables.
coverage_gap_note: This method increases water loss from canals, which could negatively impact water availability for tail-end farmers in the irrigation system.

promise_text_en: To prevent soil erosion and strengthen the banks of canals and water bodies, trees such as palm, pongamia, portia, bamboo, neem, and tamarind will be planted. Bathing ghats will also be constructed for public use.
promise_text_ta: கால்வாய் மற்றும் நீர்நிலைகளின் கரைகளில் மண் அரிப்பைத் தடுக்கவும், கரையை வலிமைப்படுத்தவும் பனை, புங்கை, பூவரசு, மூங்கில், வேம்பு, புளி போன்ற மரங்கள் நடப்படும். மக்கள் பயன்பாட்டிற்காகப் படித்துறைகள் அமைக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 63
impact_mechanism: Reduces soil erosion, strengthens riverbanks with natural vegetation, and improves public access to water bodies.

promise_components.component: Plant trees (palm, pongamia, etc.) on the banks of canals and water bodies.
promise_components.analysis: An afforestation program. The scale (length of banks, number of trees) is not defined, so the cost is not quantifiable.

promise_components.component: Construct bathing ghats for public use.
promise_components.analysis: A public works project. The number and location of ghats are not specified, so the cost is not quantifiable.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The scope of tree planting and ghat construction is undefined, making cost estimation impossible.
sustainability_reasoning: Planting trees is a long-term, natural solution to soil erosion, a root cause of bank degradation and siltation. Building ghats creates permanent community assets.
coverage_gap_note: The promise does not include provisions for the long-term maintenance of the trees or the cleanliness of the ghats.

promise_text_en: The government will maintain river basins with the cooperation of all stakeholders and government-affiliated organizations in all river basins.
promise_text_ta: அரசு, எல்லா ஆற்றுப்படுகைகளில் உள்ள பங்கீட்டாளர்கள் மற்றும் அரசு சார்ந்த நிறுவனங்கள் ஆகியோரின் ஒத்துழைப்பு மூலம் ஆற்றுப்படுகைகளைப் பேணிக்காக்கும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 63
impact_mechanism: Improves the governance and sustainability of water resources by involving local communities and organizations in management.

promise_components.component: Maintain river basins with the cooperation of all stakeholders and government-affiliated organizations.
promise_components.analysis: A governance reform promise to create a participatory management model. The cost is administrative (facilitating meetings, forming committees) and not quantifiable.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. The costs are administrative and likely a small part of departmental budgets.
sustainability_reasoning: This addresses a root cause of resource degradation: top-down management that lacks local buy-in. A participatory model is a structural change in governance intended to produce more sustainable outcomes.
coverage_gap_note: Ensuring that 'stakeholders' truly includes marginalized and less powerful groups can be a significant challenge in implementation.

promise_text_en: Water management officials will be trained. Water scarcity in river basins will be addressed by accurately calculating water availability and demand through engineering-based management methods.
promise_text_ta: நீர் மேலாண்மை அதிகாரிகளுக்குப் பயிற்சி அளிக்கப்படும். பொறியியல் சார்ந்த மேலாண்மை வழிகளில் தண்ணீர் இருப்பு, அதன் தேவை போன்றவற்றைத் துல்லியமாகக் கணக்கிட்டு ஒரு ஆற்றுப்படுகையில் நிலவும் தண்ணீர்ப் பற்றாக்குறை களையப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 63
impact_mechanism: Improves the state's technical capacity for water management by training officials and adopting data-driven methods.

promise_components.component: Train water management officials.
promise_components.analysis: A human resource development initiative. The cost depends on the number of officials and the nature of the training, which is not specified.

promise_components.component: Use engineering-based management methods to calculate water availability and demand.
promise_components.analysis: A shift to a more scientific approach in water management. This involves adopting new protocols and potentially new technology. Costs are not quantifiable.

fiscal_cost_note: Uncosted. Training costs would be part of departmental administrative budgets and are likely to be modest.
sustainability_reasoning: Investing in the skills of personnel and improving institutional processes is a structural improvement. It addresses the root cause of poor management that stems from a lack of technical capacity and data.
coverage_gap_note: Training alone is not sufficient; officials must also be empowered with the mandate and tools to apply their new skills.

promise_text_en: New lakes and 'kanmais' with areas of 500 and 1000 'kurukkam' will be created in the names of our great ancestors, and feeder canals will be dug for them.
promise_text_ta: பெரும்பாட்டன்களின் பெயரில் 500 மற்றும் 1000 குறுக்கம் பரப்பளவில் புதிய ஏரிகள், கண்மாய்கள் உருவாக்கப்படும். அதற்கான நீர்வரத்துக் கால்வாய்கள் வெட்டப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 64
impact_mechanism: This promise aims to increase surface water storage capacity by creating new, large water bodies and the channels to fill them.

promise_components.component: Create new lakes and 'kanmais' with specified areas (500 and 1000 'kurukkam').
promise_components.analysis: The unit 'kurukkam' is not standard and its conversion to acres or hectares is not provided. The cost of land acquisition, excavation, and construction for new water bodies is not available in the reference data. Therefore, a cost estimate cannot be calculated.

promise_components.component: Dig feeder canals for the new water bodies.
promise_components.analysis: The length, number, and cost per unit length for digging new canals are not available in the reference data. Therefore, a cost estimate cannot be calculated.

fiscal_cost_note: The cost cannot be calculated as there is no data on land acquisition costs, excavation costs, or the size of a 'kurukkam'. This would be a major capital expenditure project.
sustainability_reasoning: This directly addresses water scarcity by creating new, permanent water storage infrastructure. It is a long-term solution to improve water availability and groundwater recharge, tackling the root cause of water shortages.
coverage_gap_note: Benefits are localized to the regions where these new water bodies are constructed.

promise_text_en: Trees like golden shower, banyan, portia, peepal, teak, and plants like vetiver and reeds will be planted around water bodies, which will be transformed and protected to be suitable for the ecosystem and biodiversity.
promise_text_ta: நீர் நிலைகளின் கரைகளைச் சுற்றி கனிக்கொன்றை, ஆலமரம், பூவரசு, அரசமரம், தேக்குமரம், கோங்குமரம், கடம்ப மரம், மருத மரம், வெட்டி வேர், நாணல் மற்றும் கோரைகள் நட்டு வளர்க்கப்பட்டு, உயிர்ச்சூழல், பல்லுயிர்ப் பெருக்கத்திற்கு ஏற்றாற்போல் நீர் நிலைகள் மாற்றப்பட்டுப் பாதுகாக்கப்படும்.
source_notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal OCR + translation on pages 65
